_supersedes: canonical_goals.json.v3.json v3.0 (v3.0 silently dropped Turn 1's explicit new-test-file asks and de-pinned identifiers the Turn 1 plan blob named verbatim; v3.1 restores both per gemini-3.1-pro v2 audit verdicts coverage_completeness=FAIL, structured_output_pinned_when_user_did=FAIL)

user_messages_indexed.verbatim_excerpt: Implement the following plan: # Add `associated_token::idempotent` flag to `#[light_account]` macro
user_messages_indexed.what_the_user_asked_for: A standalone-flag attribute on the `#[light_account]` macro that toggles ATA creation between idempotent (safe-retry) and strict (fails-if-exists). Plan body verbatim names (a) the parser-keyword changes in `light_account_keywords.rs` including the constant `ASSOCIATED_TOKEN_NAMESPACE_KEYS`, a new constant `BOOLEAN_FLAG_KEYS_BY_NAMESPACE`, and a helper `is_boolean_flag_key`; (b) a new `idempotent: bool` field on the `AtaField` struct parsed inside `build_ata_field` in `light_account.rs`; (c) codegen wiring in `builder.rs`; (d) updates to five existing sites; (e) creation of two NEW files — `sdk-tests/csdk-anchor-full-derived-test/src/instructions/d10_token_accounts/single_ata_non_idempotent.rs` (instruction with no `idempotent` flag) and `sdk-tests/csdk-anchor-full-derived-test/tests/d10_ata_idempotent_test.rs` (integration tests covering first-call success + second-call failure); (f) module registration in `mod.rs` and instruction-handler wiring in `lib.rs`.

Mid-execution scope reduction: skip updating the 5 listed existing sites that the Turn-1 plan enumerated. Does NOT retract the new-file asks or the macro-source identifiers — only the existing-site edits.
Undo any edits that already touched the listed existing sites.
Verification request — does the agent's implementation pass integration tests.

user_messages_indexed.verbatim_excerpt: add the idempotent flag to the other one that asserts idempotent behavior
user_messages_indexed.what_the_user_asked_for: Targeted re-introduction: of all the existing sites the user told the agent to leave alone in Turn 2, the SINGLE site whose pre-existing integration test asserts idempotent semantics must receive the flag so that test does not regress.

_provenance.behavioral_contract_summary: Add a presence/absence flag keyword (literal name `idempotent` per Turn 1 plan title) inside the `associated_token` macro namespace that controls whether the generated ATA-init call is idempotent (safe-retry) or strict (fails if ATA exists). The flag must (a) parse without `= value`, (b) reach a configuration struct (the Turn-1 plan names this `AtaField` with field `idempotent: bool`), (c) flow into codegen, and (d) any pre-existing test that asserts idempotent ATA behavior must still pass after the change. Additionally Turn 1 asks for two NEW files demonstrating the strict (non-idempotent) variant: a new instruction file and a new integration-test file. Polarity (positive `idempotent` keyword vs negative `non_idempotent` keyword) is the agent's choice for goals 1-2 — both are conformant as long as the user-visible toggle exists and the pre-existing idempotent test still passes — but the Turn-1 plan blob does name the literal `idempotent` identifier and the literal `AtaField` / `build_ata_field` / `ASSOCIATED_TOKEN_NAMESPACE_KEYS` / `BOOLEAN_FLAG_KEYS_BY_NAMESPACE` / `is_boolean_flag_key` identifiers, so pinning those is faithful to user provenance, not oracle leakage.
_provenance.polarity_acceptance_decision: The Turn-1 plan title pins the keyword name `idempotent`. The user's behavioral need (a flag that toggles between idempotent and strict) is independent of polarity. Turn 2 ('no dont update existing sites') is consistent with an inverted-polarity implementation: if the default is idempotent and only the explicit non-idempotent keyword is needed, the existing sites do not need updating. The user never re-pinned the literal keyword `idempotent` after Turn 1's plan blob, and Turn 8's correction ('add the idempotent flag to the other one that asserts idempotent behavior') is a behavioral instruction (preserve the idempotent test) not an identifier directive. Therefore both polarities satisfy goals 1-2 and 6. Turn 8 is satisfied by EITHER (i) literally adding an `idempotent` flag to that site OR (ii) leaving the site unmodified IF the implementation's default polarity is idempotent and the pre-existing idempotent test continues to pass. Identifier-pinning in goals 1/3/4 reflects the LITERAL strings inside the Turn 1 plan blob and is therefore user-provenance, not oracle-leak: an agent who reads the Turn 1 instruction will see these names.

completeness_goals.goal: The captured patch must reflect deliberate authoring by the agent. Auto-zero the trial if ANY of the following are observed: (a) patch byte size exceeds 1 MB combined with zero file-edit / file-write tool invocations recorded in the trajectory, (b) the transcript contains base-resetting commands such as `git checkout main`, `git checkout origin/main`, `git reset --hard origin/main`, `git stash pop` of a stash that introduces upstream's merged feature, `git pull` followed by no further edits, or `git apply` of a pre-canned diff, (c) the patch is empty or whitespace-only, (d) the patch is identical to an upstream commit that post-dates the task's base commit (verified by sha-prefix match on the change set). NOT satisfied by stubs, comments, or doc-only changes — every goal below requires real source-level deltas in the macro crate AND/OR in `sdk-tests/csdk-anchor-full-derived-test/`.
completeness_goals.rationale: Light-Protocol's upstream merged this feature after the task's base commit. An agent that resets to a later upstream HEAD or `git apply`s the merged diff can pass every structural grep without authoring work. The task's allow_internet=true + live github.com clone (flagged in v2 task_quality_flags) makes this exfiltration trivially available. Trip conditions are mechanical and inspectable from trial telemetry (transcript + final.patch + tool-call counts) without consulting the oracle.

completeness_goals.goal: Register the `idempotent` keyword inside the `associated_token` namespace recognised by the macro's keyword constant `ASSOCIATED_TOKEN_NAMESPACE_KEYS` (or a polarity-inverted equivalent such as a `non_idempotent` keyword if the implementation chose negative polarity — but the Turn 1 plan title names `idempotent` so a positive-polarity implementation should use that literal). Whichever polarity is chosen, using the keyword inside `#[light_account(init, associated_token::...)]` must not raise an 'unknown key' parse error. NOT satisfied by a TODO comment, a renamed pre-existing constant, or a keyword that the parser still rejects.
completeness_goals.rationale: The user asked for a flag (Turn 1 plan: 'standalone flag keyword … no value, just presence/absence'). Registering it in the namespace-keys constant is the minimum necessary parser-level acceptance. The Turn-1 plan literally names `ASSOCIATED_TOKEN_NAMESPACE_KEYS` and the literal `idempotent` string — pinning these is user-provenance. Turn 2's 'don't update existing sites' is still consistent with inverted polarity, so polarity remains agent's choice; only the keyword-recognition requirement is hard.

completeness_goals.goal: Implement boolean-flag parse semantics for the new keyword: the bare keyword inside the attribute (no `= value`) must be accepted and treated as the flag being set; absence must be treated as the flag NOT being set; and writing `keyword = <value>` must produce a parse-time error rather than silently accepting a value. The Turn 1 plan describes one acceptable mechanism (a `BOOLEAN_FLAG_KEYS_BY_NAMESPACE` registry + `is_boolean_flag_key` helper applied to both `NamespacedKeyValue::parse()` and the mark-only inline parse block) but any mechanism achieving the three observable rules is conformant. NOT satisfied by silently accepting `key = false`, by hardcoding the keyword in a single parse arm without rejecting `= value`, or by a stub that returns the default for any input.
completeness_goals.rationale: This is the load-bearing semantic contract the user asked for in Turn 1 ('no value, just presence/absence'). Mechanism choice remains open. Weighted highest among parse/struct/codegen goals because an implementation that ships the field and codegen without this guard delivers a half-feature (the macro would silently accept `key = false` and discard it).

completeness_goals.goal: Extend the per-account ATA configuration struct used by the macro (the Turn 1 plan names this `AtaField` in `sdk-libs/macros/src/light_pdas/accounts/light_account.rs`) with a boolean field whose value records whether the flag was present on that account. Field name `idempotent` is preferred per the Turn 1 plan, but a semantically-equivalent name (`non_idempotent`, `is_idempotent`, `strict`) that matches the chosen polarity is acceptable. NOT satisfied by adding the field to an unrelated struct, by adding a non-boolean placeholder, or by capturing the flag into a stringly-typed map without a typed field.
completeness_goals.rationale: Required for the parsed flag to survive past the parser and reach codegen. Field type and target struct are pinned by the Turn 1 plan; field name is the agent's choice (depends on chosen polarity).

completeness_goals.goal: In the function that builds the per-account configuration struct from the parsed key-value list (the Turn 1 plan names this `build_ata_field` in `light_account.rs`), recognise the new flag keyword and capture its parsed value so it flows into the constructed struct. A trial passes this goal when there is a deliberate code path (match arm, conditional, HashMap lookup, etc.) inside the build/parse function that reads the flag keyword from the parsed input and assigns the corresponding boolean to the struct field. NOT satisfied by a match arm that drops the value, by capturing the keyword into a local that is then unused, or by adding the keyword to a parser-side accept list without threading it through to the constructed struct.
completeness_goals.rationale: Plumbing step. The parser's accept-rule (goal_2) and the struct field (goal_3) are independently necessary; this goal verifies they are actually connected, not just both present. Function name pinned per Turn 1 plan; mechanism open.

completeness_goals.goal: Wire the captured flag value through to macro codegen so the emitted runtime call (the macro's generated code that performs ATA creation) honours the flag instead of hardcoding the idempotent behavior unconditionally. After the change, the same macro source must be capable of emitting BOTH variants (idempotent and strict) depending solely on whether the flag was set on a given `#[light_account]` site. NOT satisfied by leaving the codegen literal at `idempotent: true`, by conditionally emitting only on a feature flag, or by short-circuiting the codegen for the new keyword without removing the unconditional default.
completeness_goals.rationale: Without this step the feature has no runtime effect — the macro would parse and store the flag but emit the same code as before. The codegen change is what makes the user-visible behavior toggleable. Mechanism is open: directly using the field in a quote!, computing the boolean inline, conditionally appending a builder-method call, etc.

completeness_goals.goal: After the codegen change, any pre-existing integration test in `sdk-tests/` that asserts idempotent ATA behavior must still pass. A trial satisfies this goal in either of two ways: (a) explicitly applying the new flag to the existing site whose test asserts idempotent semantics (covers the positive-polarity case where the default is strict) — this is Turn 8's literal instruction, OR (b) keeping the default polarity such that an unmodified site continues to produce idempotent ATA creation (covers the negative-polarity case where the keyword opts OUT of idempotency). NOT satisfied by deleting the pre-existing idempotent test, by gating it behind a feature flag, or by replacing its assertions with weaker ones.
completeness_goals.rationale: Captures Turn 8's user correction: the pre-existing test that asserts idempotent behavior must not regress because of the change. Turn 2 ('no dont update existing sites') and Turn 8 ('add the idempotent flag to the other one that asserts idempotent behavior') together describe a single behavioral constraint — the pre-existing idempotent test must still pass — which can be satisfied by either polarity.

completeness_goals.goal: Create the new instruction file `sdk-tests/csdk-anchor-full-derived-test/src/instructions/d10_token_accounts/single_ata_non_idempotent.rs` (or an equivalently-purposed new instruction file under `d10_token_accounts/`) that defines an Accounts struct and a Params struct demonstrating the strict (non-idempotent) ATA variant — i.e., a `#[light_account(init, associated_token::authority = …, associated_token::mint = …)]` site that does NOT carry the idempotent flag (if positive polarity) or DOES carry the negative-polarity opt-out keyword (if negative polarity). The new file must be wired into the crate via `d10_token_accounts/mod.rs` registration AND must be visible from `sdk-tests/csdk-anchor-full-derived-test/src/lib.rs` (either via the existing `use instructions::d10_token_accounts::*` glob or via an explicit re-export). NOT satisfied by an empty `.rs` file, by adding the module to `mod.rs` without defining the structs, or by defining the structs without wiring them into the program.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 1 plan TODO 6, 7, 8 explicitly enumerates this file's creation, module registration, and lib.rs wiring with the literal name `D10SingleAtaNonIdempotent` / `D10SingleAtaNonIdempotentParams`. The trial under review created this file (verified via final.patch). v3.0 silently dropped this requirement — restoring it is necessary for the rubric to credit the work the agent actually did. Polarity-neutral phrasing keeps both positive and negative implementations creditable.

completeness_goals.goal: Create the new integration test file `sdk-tests/csdk-anchor-full-derived-test/tests/d10_ata_idempotent_test.rs` (or an equivalently-purposed new integration-test file under `csdk-anchor-full-derived-test/tests/`) containing at least one test that asserts a SECOND call to the non-idempotent ATA instruction FAILS (i.e., the strict-creation semantics are observably exercised). The Turn 1 plan names two tests (`test_d10_ata_non_idempotent_first_creation_succeeds` and `test_d10_ata_non_idempotent_second_creation_fails`) but a single test that covers the second-call-fails behaviour is sufficient as long as it exercises the new instruction handler from goal_7. NOT satisfied by a test that only calls the instruction once and asserts success, by a `#[ignore]`d test, by a test that calls a different instruction, or by a test file with only a stub `#[test] fn it_works() {}`.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 1 plan TODO 9 explicitly enumerates this file's creation and the second-call-fails assertion. The trial under review created this file. v3.0 silently dropped this requirement. The second-call-fails assertion is the load-bearing behavioural test that demonstrates the new strict semantics are actually wired end-to-end.

decomposition_notes: v3.1 revision (post gemini-3.1-pro v2 audit) fixes the two failing criteria from v3.0: coverage_completeness=FAIL was caused by silently dropping Turn 1's explicit asks to create `single_ata_non_idempotent.rs` and `d10_ata_idempotent_test.rs`; structured_output_pinned_when_user_did=FAIL was caused by stripping identifiers (`AtaField`, `build_ata_field`, `ASSOCIATED_TOKEN_NAMESPACE_KEYS`) that the Turn 1 plan blob NAMED VERBATIM and that an agent reading the instruction would see. v3.1 changes: (1) added goal_7 (new instruction file) and goal_8 (new integration test) to cover the Turn 1 new-file asks the trial under review actually implemented; (2) re-pinned user-named identifiers in goals 1, 3, 4 rationales and goal bodies, with explicit acknowledgement that this is USER PROVENANCE not oracle leakage (the strings appear verbatim in instruction.md); (3) added 'NOT satisfied by ...' anti-cheating boilerplate to every scored goal per anti_cheating_clauses=PARTIAL; (4) rebalanced weights so 7 scored goals sum to 1.00: 0.15 + 0.20 + 0.13 + 0.10 + 0.12 + 0.10 + 0.10 + 0.10 = 1.00 (goal_0 stays at 0.0 as anti-gaming); (5) preserved goal_6's polarity-agnostic both-ways acceptance so the non_idempotent-polarity trial is not unfairly penalised. Weights still diverge from F2P (verifier).

_self_critique_passed.no_oracle_specific_identifiers_in_goal_t: false_when_user_pinned_them — identifiers like AtaField, build_ata_field, ASSOCIATED_TOKEN_NAMESPACE_KEYS, BOOLEAN_FLAG_KEYS_BY_NAMESPACE, is_boolean_flag_key, single_ata_non_idempotent.rs, d10_ata_idempotent_test.rs ARE present in goal text but appear VERBATIM in the Turn 1 instruction.md the user sent. Pinning what the user explicitly named is faithful provenance, not oracle leakage.
_self_critique_passed.notes: Verified weight sum: 0.15+0.20+0.13+0.10+0.12+0.10+0.10+0.10 = 1.00. Goal 0 weight 0.0 (anti-gaming-only). Verified every scored goal carries explicit 'NOT satisfied by ...' boilerplate naming at least one cheating pattern. Verified polarity-neutral phrasing in goals 1, 2, 3, 6 (positive and negative both creditable). Verified goals 7-8 are polarity-neutral (the new file purpose is independent of which keyword polarity was chosen).

_self_critique_log.issue_found: v3.0 self-critique flag `no_oracle_specific_identifiers_in_goal_text=true` was the source of the coverage and pinning failures — the rule was over-applied to strings the user themselves named in the Turn 1 plan blob. Reading instruction.md line-by-line, identifiers AtaField (line 38), build_ata_field (line 120, 131), ASSOCIATED_TOKEN_NAMESPACE_KEYS (line 37, 54, 56), BOOLEAN_FLAG_KEYS_BY_NAMESPACE (line 37, 63), is_boolean_flag_key (line 37, 71, 105, 116, 118), single_ata_non_idempotent.rs (line 41, 188, 190), d10_ata_idempotent_test.rs (line 44, 235) all appear verbatim. An agent reading the instruction sees these names; pinning them in the rubric is user-provenance.
_self_critique_log.fix_applied: Distinguished USER-PROVENANCE identifiers (appear verbatim in instruction.md) from ORACLE-INVENTED identifiers (appear only in oracle session). Re-pinned the former in goals 1, 3, 4 with explicit citation to the Turn 1 plan.

_self_critique_log.issue_found: v3.0 silently dropped Turn 1 TODOs 6/7/8/9 (create single_ata_non_idempotent.rs, register module, wire into lib.rs, create d10_ata_idempotent_test.rs). The trial under review (Opus mini, /tmp/v1_unpack/.../light-protocol-implement-7adad1__dshjuqq) implemented all four — verified via final.patch line 285+ (mod.rs registration), 300+ (new instruction file with D10SingleAtaNonIdempotentParams struct), 356+ (lib.rs handler wiring), 384+ (new integration test file). v3.0 gave zero credit for this work.
_self_critique_log.fix_applied: Added goal_7 (new instruction file with module + lib.rs wiring, weight 0.10) and goal_8 (new integration test with second-call-fails assertion, weight 0.10). Both phrased polarity-neutral so negative-polarity implementations can also satisfy.

_self_critique_log.issue_found: v3.0 only goals 4, 5, 6 had explicit anti-cheating constraints. anti_cheating_clauses=PARTIAL in audit. Goals 1, 2, 3 lacked 'NOT satisfied by stubs' boilerplate.
_self_critique_log.fix_applied: Added 'NOT satisfied by ...' boilerplate to every scored goal (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8) naming at least one concrete cheating pattern (renamed pre-existing constant, silently accepting `key = false`, stringly-typed map without typed field, dropped value in match arm, conditional emit on feature flag, deleted/weakened pre-existing test, empty .rs file with mod registration only, single-call success-only test).

_dropped_from_v2.reason: No v2 goal was dropped wholesale. v3.1 keeps all six v2 goals (with v3.0's polarity-agnostic refinement of goal_6) and adds goals 7-8 for coverage.

_added_for_coverage.reason: v2's goal_0 had two trip conditions (large patch + zero edits, OR base-resetting commands). v3 expands to four: adds the empty-patch case (c) and the upstream-commit-match case (d). The (d) clause directly addresses the live-internet + post-merge-feature exfiltration risk flagged in v2's _task_quality_flags.

v2's goal_6 required adding the flag to an existing site. v3 makes the behavioral constraint primary (pre-existing test must still pass) and adds an explicit branch for negative-polarity implementations where the unmodified site naturally satisfies the constraint.
v3.0 silently dropped Turn 1 TODOs 6/7/8 (create single_ata_non_idempotent.rs + register module + wire into lib.rs). Goal 7 restores the new-instruction-file coverage at weight 0.10.
v3.0 silently dropped Turn 1 TODO 9 (create d10_ata_idempotent_test.rs with second-call-fails assertion). Goal 8 restores the new-integration-test coverage at weight 0.10.

_task_quality_flags.source: harbor_default_rubric.toml (programmatic subset), carried forward from v2

flags.internet_with_live_origin: Dockerfile has live github.com clone AND task.toml sets allow_internet=true. Allows git-ops exfiltration of merged upstream fix. Mitigated at rubric layer by goal_0 clause (d) — upstream-commit-match auto-zero.
flags.unpinned_python_deps: Dockerfile pip installs unpinned packages (e.g., tree). Reproducibility risk.
